ideas.title: Context-Aware Dynamic Sensitivity Analysis for Robust Parameter Influence Mapping
ideas.description: This approach moves beyond a single, static global sensitivity index by quantifying how parameter influence on ΔR varies across different data contexts and model operational regimes. It performs stratified sensitivity analysis to compute conditional Sobol indices within coherent data segments and employs a meta-model to predict sensitivity shifts. This results in a robust, transferable map of parameter importance that explicitly accounts for the non-stationarity of complex systems, ensuring findings about critical parameters are valid across the diverse conditions the model will encounter.

ideas.title: Causally Regularized ML for Robust Event Activity Classification
ideas.description: This approach trains machine learning models to identify an event activity variable that minimizes nonflow effects while explicitly guarding against learning spurious correlations. It integrates adversarial neural networks to penalize the model for using information from known background or detector-specific artifacts, enforcing a separation between the learned variable and non-causal factors. The system is validated through causal intervention tests on high-fidelity simulated data where true background levels are systematically varied. This results in a physically interpretable classifier whose performance generalizes across collision energies and detector configurations, providing a robust measure of event activity.

ideas.title: Joint Causal Discovery and Robust Effect Estimation for Event Activity Variable Selection
ideas.description: This research addresses the core flaw of assuming a known causal model by developing a unified framework that first learns the probable causal structure from observational event data. It then performs robust causal inference across the space of plausible models to estimate the effect of choosing a specific event activity variable, explicitly accounting for structural uncertainty. The output is not a single effect estimate but a distribution of effects conditioned on the causal knowledge derived from the data, providing a rigorous measure of confidence for the variable selection decision. This approach makes the idea applicable to real-world scenarios where the causal graph is unknown and must be discovered from the data itself.

ideas.title: Physics-Constrained Deep Unmixing for Disentangling Skin and Deformation Spectra
ideas.description: This adversarially improved system combines the flexibility of deep learning with the rigor of biophysical models to separate spectroscopic signals. A neural network performs the source separation, but its training and architecture are constrained by an embedded, differentiable model of tissue optics that simulates expected spectral changes from both physiological and mechanical perturbations. This ensures the separated 'skin' and 'deformation' components are not just statistically independent but are also consistent with known photophysical principles. The result is a more robust and interpretable decomposition that can generalize better across individuals and measurement conditions.

ideas.title: Adapting Ecological Niche Sensitivity Analysis for AI Safety
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from Ecology and Conservation Biology, where global sensitivity analysis (e.g., Sobol indices) is used to quantify the influence of numerous interacting environmental parameters on critical outcomes like a species' range shift (ΔRange). The analogous solution involves applying these methods to complex, non-linear ecological models to pinpoint key drivers for conservation prioritization. Adapted to the AI safety research context, the method involves constructing a meta-model or simulation of an AI system's learning or deployment process, where the outcome of interest is the behavioral shift ΔR. By treating training hyperparameters, architectural choices, and data characteristics as input factors, a variance-based global sensitivity analysis can be performed. This will rigorously decompose the variance in ΔR to compute Sobol indices, identifying which parameters and their interactions are most influential in causing ΔR. This provides a principled, quantitative method to move beyond heuristic understanding and prioritize robustness and alignment efforts on the parameters to which ΔR is most sensitive.

ideas.title: Applying Mendelian Randomization to Event Activity Selection
ideas.description: This research adapts the causal inference solution of Mendelian Randomization from epidemiology to the problem of event activity selection. In epidemiology, Mendelian Randomization uses genetic instruments to estimate the causal effect of an exposure. Analogously, in an event-driven system (e.g., web analytics, sensor networks), we can use a randomly assigned, pre-treatment system variable (e.g., a user or session identifier with specific properties) as an instrumental variable. This instrument influences which event activity variable (e.g., 'click count' vs. 'dwell time') is prioritized for a given unit of analysis but is otherwise independent of the outcome. By applying this instrumental variable framework, we can rigorously define and estimate the true causal effect of selecting one activity metric over another on a key outcome, isolating this effect from confounding by user behavior or system state.
